# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { ReadableStream } from 'stream';  // Import the necessary library to handle streams (Readable Stream)
const crypto = require('crypto');         // Require for hashing and encryption functions in NodeJS environment, you can use any other libraries as well.
export const config = {                     // Define your Vercel edge function's configuration here 
    api: {                              // The API endpoint where the request will be made to when a client makes an HTTP POST Request (e.g., /api/stream)  
        bodyParser: false,               // Disable default parsing of JSON bodies for security reasons and use rawBody option instead which allows you read directly from 'body' in your function 
    },                                   // Define the configuration here as per Vercel documentation on how to set up API endpoints. You can also define headers or query parameters if needed, but this is not necessary with a stream response (ReadableStream).  
};                                     // End of config definition section    
export default function(request: any): ReadableStream {  // Define your Vercel edge functions's main logic here as per the requirement. This should return an instance of 'stream'. You can use NodeJS streams or other libraries to handle this, but for simplicity and readability we are using a stream in our example
    const body = request.rawBody;           // Get raw POST data from Vercel's Request object (request) 
    
   if (!body || typeof(body)!== 'string') {      // Check whether the user-provided content is valid or not, and use secure platform APIs for validation as per requirement. This can be done using any other libraries/APIs provided by your system like crypto (for hashing), etc
        throw new Error('Invalid request body');  // If invalid data found then return an error message to client with appropriate HTTP status code, you may also want to log the issue for further debugging.  
    }                                       // End of validation section    
      const hash = crypto.createHash("sha256").update(body).digest('hex');  // Create a SHA-256 Hash from user provided content, this can be used as unique identifier or any other sensitive data for your API  
    return new ReadableStream({                     // Return an instance of 'Readable Stream' with the necessary logic to stream back responses using Vercel’s Response object (response)  and Web streams. This will enable real-time streaming at edge computing level, where user provided content is directly piped into response
        start(controller: any){                     // Start method for ReadStream instance which gets called when a client opens the stream connection to your function  
            controller.enqueue('data:'+hash);       // Enqueues data (user-provided information) and sends it as is, no further processing required here 
        }                                         // End of start section    
    });                                             // Return ReadableStream instance with the necessary logic to stream back responses using Vercel’s Response object. This will enable real time streaming at edge computing level  
}                                                     //End function definition and export statement;                                                                      };
